architecture.summary: vLLM Semantic Router is a signal-driven decision routing framework for Mixture-of-Modality (MoM) model deployments. The system orchestrates thirteen heterogeneous signal types (sub-millisecond heuristics and neural classifiers for semantics, safety, and modality) through configurable Boolean decision rules into routing policies. Signal extraction reduces entropy of model selection by distilling routing-relevant information from queries. A typed neural-symbolic DSL specifies routing policies and compiles them to deployment targets. Matched decisions drive semantic model routing via thirteen selection algorithms, with per-decision plugin chains enforcing safety constraints including a three-stage HaluGate hallucination detection pipeline and an episodic memory system (ReflectionGate) for personalized multi-turn context.
trained_components: neural classifiers for semantics safety and modality

biblio.title: vLLM Semantic Router: Signal Driven Decision Routing for Mixture-of-Modality Models

claims.statement: Composable signal orchestration enables thirteen heterogeneous signal types, spanning sub-millisecond heuristics and neural classifiers for semantics, safety, and modality, to be composed through configurable Boolean decision rules into deployment-specific routing policies

Matched decisions drive semantic model routing via thirteen selection algorithms, while per-decision plugin chains enforce safety constraints including a three-stage HaluGate hallucination detection pipeline and a lightweight episodic memory system with ReflectionGate for personalized multi-turn context
A typed neural-symbolic DSL specifies routing policies and compiles them to multiple deployment targets, enabling configuration-first adaptation without code changes

The architecture follows two complementary Shannon-inspired views: in the information-theoretic regime, signal extraction reduces the entropy of 'which model?' by distilling routing-relevant information from raw queries; in the Boolean-algebraic regime, the decision engine composes functionally complete routing policies from signal conditions
The framework serves diverse deployment scenarios with differentiated cost, privacy, and safety policies through configuration-based adaptation over a single architecture
